touched_files.description: TestReaderDuringSplit: replaced fixed-iteration sleep-based reader loop's sole reliance on timing with a deterministic barrier (existing atomicCommitHook seam) plus one guaranteed-post-rename read.

files_explicitly_not_touched_per_scope: engine/split/content.go (does not exist in current tree)

dependents.engine/split: TestReaderDuringSplit exercises catalog.ContentStore.Read, split.Orchestrator.BeginSplit/AbortSplit, split.ExecuteSplitAtomic -- all read-only usage from the test's perspective; no API surface changed.
dependents.engine/split_execute_hook: Reuses the pre-existing atomicCommitHook package var (already shared with execute_test.go's TestSplitAtomicCommit); mutated only for this test's duration and restored to nil via defer. No t.Parallel() in the package (confirmed via grep), so no concurrent-test interference risk.

risk_notes: Test-only change; zero production code modified.
Reuses an existing, already-vetted test seam (atomicCommitHook) rather than introducing new production surface.

Background reader loop bound changed from fixed count (400) to a stop-channel-terminated loop with a defensive 2,000,000-iteration cap; cannot spin materially longer than before in practice since it is stopped immediately after the split completes.
No API, schema, or WAL format changes.
